content.question: Would abolishing or delaying the 6- and 8-year gimnazium tracks reduce Hungary's socio-economic segregation if primary-school choice mechanisms remain unreformed?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a quasi-experimental pilot comparing schools in regions that delay gimnazium selection while holding primary-school choice rules constant versus regions with both interventions; measure within-region segregation before and after tracking change to isolate its independent effect from school-choice segregation
